title: What makes an audit trail trustworthy
description: A compliance-grade trail is complete, attributable, time-ordered, and tamper-evident, which ordinary application logs are not.
body: Use when deciding whether existing logging satisfies an audit requirement. A trustworthy trail is complete (every controlled action is recorded), attributable (tied to a specific authenticated principal, not a shared service account), time-ordered with a reliable clock, and tamper-evident (alterations are blocked or detectable). Ordinary application logs usually fail one or more: they drop events under load, use shared identities, or live in a store the app can edit and rotate. Do not equate having logs with having an audit trail. Actions: check each property and inspect the write path and permissions. Validation signals include a test proving each controlled action emits exactly one attributable, immutable record. Risk boundary: a trail missing attribution or tamper-evidence cannot be relied on for an assessment; escalate.
